card.intuition: Solve a problem on intervals [i, j] by trying every way to split the interval at some point k, combining the answers for [i, k] and [k+1, j]. Fill the table by increasing interval length — smaller intervals are always filled before larger ones that contain them.
card.analogy: Evaluating a mathematical expression by trying every possible place to apply the last operation. The answer for the full expression depends on the answers for all sub-expressions — which were already computed when the interval was shorter.
card.time_complexity: O(n³) — n² intervals × n split points
when_to_use: Matrix chain multiplication (split at which multiplication to do last)
Burst Balloons (split at which balloon to pop last)
Optimal Binary Search Tree, polygon triangulation
gotchas: Fill by INCREASING LENGTH — dp[i][j] of length l must be filled after all sub-intervals of length < l
The split k can be inclusive of either endpoint depending on the problem — read the recurrence carefully
Interval DP is O(n³) — with Knuth's optimisation, some variants drop to O(n²)

exercise.prompt: Burst Balloons: given balloons with values, burst them to maximise coins. Coins = nums[left] * nums[i] * nums[right] where left/right are the nearest un-burst neighbours.
exercise.starter_code:
def max_coins(nums):
    # Pad with sentinel 1s at both ends
    nums = [1] + nums + [1]
    n = len(nums)
    dp = [[0] * n for _ in range(n)]

    # dp[i][j] = max coins bursting all balloons strictly between i and j
    for length in range(2, n):
        for i in range(0, n - length):
            j = i + length
            for k in range(i + 1, j):  # k is the LAST balloon burst in (i, j)
                coins = nums[i] * nums[k] * nums[j]
                dp[i][j] = max(dp[i][j],
                               ___________ + coins + ___________)
    return dp[0][n-1]
hints: dp[i][k] = coins from bursting balloons between i and k (excluding i and k themselves); add dp[k][j] for the right half; then add the coins for bursting k last (with i and j as its neighbours)
exercise.test_code:
assert max_coins([3,1,5,8]) == 167
assert max_coins([1,5]) == 10
assert max_coins([5]) == 5
assert max_coins([1]) == 1
assert max_coins([2,4,3,5]) == 54
print('PASS')
